headline: nemotron-super-49b-v1.5 recovered to 95.8% after weekly-limit reset — llama-3.3-70b-instruct held back
message: After the D96 correction (see below) and the weekly usage-limit reset, nemotron-super-49b-v1.5 was rerun with max_tokens=2048 and workers=4 (RateLimitedClient's sliding-window gate is thread-safe, already proven under workers=4 in the earlier 16-model retry -- the workers=1 setting from D94b was unneeded caution carried over from a different model's failure mode). Result: 23/24 (95.8%), Track B precision 1.0, clear strong/weak separation (5.0 vs 1.32) and self-correction differentiation (improving 4.5 vs weak 1.12). Re-checked the full transcript set for the weekly-limit contamination string: zero hits. llama-3.3-70b-instruct was attempted first at timeout_s=600/workers=1 -- all 9 jobs tried so far failed at 302-766s each, indicating the NVIDIA-side worker-queue overload is not a momentary blip. The user chose to defer this model rather than keep burning time on it this round.
notes: nemotron-super-49b-v1.5: root cause (reasoning model, max_tokens starvation) confirmed via raw HTTP diagnostic in D94, fix confirmed via live rerun in D97.

llama-3.3-70b-instruct: root cause (NVIDIA worker-queue overload, HTTP 503 "153/16") still stands, but recovery attempt this round was abandoned after 9/9 failures at extended timeout -- deferred to a future session.
Correction from the prior quota-contamination incident (D96) held: this rerun's successes were verified contamination-free before publishing.
See README.md D97 for the full writeup, including a module-reload bug found along the way (two independent copies of the same script module via importlib caused CLIENT=None).

meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct.top_error_summary: 9/9 attempted failed at 302-766s even with timeout_s=600 -- sustained NVIDIA worker-queue overload, not momentary. Deferred.

nvidia/llama-3.3-nemotron-super-49b-v1.5.top_error_summary: 1x remaining failure after two rounds of retry (non-timeout, cause not further diagnosed)
